Test Case Name: Valid discussion CREATE event ingested successfully
Preconditions: Kafka, Postgres, Temporal running; consumer subscribed to analytics.ingestion.raw
Test Steps: Publish a well-formed discussion CREATE event (all required fields present) to the ingestion topic
Expected Result: Submission row inserted with status 'pending'/'processing'; challenges/solutions stored as TEXT[]; no DLQ message
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka):
1) Run: python tests/kafka_push.py --file tests/kafka_events/create/create_discussion.json
2) DB: SELECT status FROM submissions WHERE submission_id='200' AND tenant_code='mitra';  -> expect 'processing' or 'success'
3) DB: SELECT challenges, solutions FROM discussion_submissions WHERE submission_id='200' AND tenant_code='mitra';  -> expect non-null TEXT[] arrays matching the fixture's statement count
4) Confirm no new message for submissionId 200 appears in the DLQ

Test Case Name: Valid story CREATE event ingested successfully
Preconditions: Kafka, Postgres, Temporal running
Test Steps: Publish a well-formed story CREATE event to the ingestion topic
Expected Result: Submission + story_submissions rows inserted correctly; no DLQ message
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka):
1) Run: python tests/kafka_push.py --file tests/kafka_events/create/create_story.json
2) DB: SELECT * FROM submissions WHERE submission_id='3280' AND tenant_code='mitra';  -> row exists, status is 'pending'/'processing'/'success'
3) DB: SELECT objective, challenge, transcript_link FROM story_submissions WHERE submission_id='3280' AND tenant_code='mitra';  -> fields populated matching the fixture
4) Confirm no DLQ message for submissionId 3280

Test Case Name: Valid UPDATE event applies delta-only newValues
Preconditions: An existing submission from a prior CREATE (run KAFKA-001 first)
Test Steps: Publish an UPDATE event with newValues containing only a subset of fields (e.g. title, participantsData)
Expected Result: Only the fields present in newValues change; all other columns (e.g. challenges, solutions) remain untouched via COALESCE
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka):
1) DB (before): SELECT title, challenges, solutions FROM discussion_submissions WHERE submission_id='200' AND tenant_code='mitra';  -> note the values
2) Run: python tests/kafka_push.py --file tests/kafka_events/update/update_discussion.json
3) DB (after): re-run the same SELECT -> title/participantsData reflect the new values from newValues; challenges/solutions are UNCHANGED from step 1
4) Confirm no DLQ message for this submissionId

Test Case Name: Valid DELETE event removes submission

Test Case Name: Malformed JSON payload routed to DLQ
Preconditions: Consumer running
Test Steps: Publish a non-JSON string (e.g. truncated/corrupt JSON) to the ingestion topic
Expected Result: json.JSONDecodeError caught; message routed to analytics.ingestion.raw.dlq with reason 'Invalid JSON: ...'; original topic offset committed
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka):
1) Use a raw producer (kafka-console-producer.sh or a short Python snippet with confluent_kafka.Producer) to publish the literal text '{bad json' (not valid JSON) to analytics.ingestion.raw
2) Consume analytics.ingestion.raw.dlq from the earliest offset (see 'Reading the DLQ' above) and locate the message by its submissionId header or by the submissionId inside the payload. reason header should start with 'Invalid JSON:'
3) DB: confirm no new submissions row was created for this message

Test Case Name: Non-object JSON payload routed to DLQ
Preconditions: Consumer running
Test Steps: Publish a valid JSON array or scalar (e.g. "[]" or "42") instead of an object
Expected Result: Routed to DLQ with reason 'Expected a JSON object, got list/int'
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka):
1) Publish the literal text '[]' (valid JSON, but not an object) to analytics.ingestion.raw
2) Consume analytics.ingestion.raw.dlq from the earliest offset (see 'Reading the DLQ' above) and locate the message by its submissionId header or by the submissionId inside the payload. reason header should read 'Expected a JSON object, got list'
3) Repeat with the literal text '42' -> reason should read '...got int'

3) DB: confirm no new submissions row was created for that sessionId

Test Case Name: DB-layer failure triggers 3 retries then DLQ
Preconditions: A submission with a colliding session_id already exists
Test Steps: Publish a CREATE event that passes ingestion validation but collides on session_id at the DB layer
Expected Result: process_message retried 3x with 2s/4s backoff on the same message; after exhausting retries, routed to DLQ with 'Processing failed after 3 attempts'; row never inserted
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka):
1) Copy a create fixture, keep an existing submission's sessionId (e.g. reuse the sessionId from submission 200) but set submissionId to a brand-new value, push it
2) App log: expect 3 'Error processing Kafka message (attempt N/3)' lines about 8 seconds apart (2s then 4s backoff), citing the session_id collision
3) Consume analytics.ingestion.raw.dlq from the earliest offset (see 'Reading the DLQ' above) and locate the message by its submissionId header or by the submissionId inside the payload. reason should read 'Processing failed after 3 attempts: ...'
4) DB: confirm no submissions row exists for the new submissionId

Test Case Name: DLQ delivery failure leaves the offset uncommitted
Preconditions: DLQ producer/topic made unreachable (simulated)
Test Steps: Force a message to fail processing and force the subsequent DLQ publish to fail
Expected Result: Offset is NOT committed; message is redelivered on next poll/restart instead of being silently dropped or skipped
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka):
1) With the consumer running, temporarily block/rename the DLQ topic (or revoke produce ACL) so publishing to it fails
2) Push a message guaranteed to fail processing (e.g. KAFKA-020's session_id collision)
3) App log should show 'Failed to route unprocessable message to DLQ — leaving offset uncommitted'
4) Restore the DLQ topic, restart the consumer, and confirm the SAME message is redelivered and this time successfully DLQ'd

Test Steps: Ingest the event and inspect the stored challenges array
Expected Result: The statement is stored as one unmodified array element (TEXT[] round-trip); not corrupted/split by any delimiter logic

Test Case Name: A later message never commits past an unresolved earlier one on the same partition
Preconditions: Two messages on the same partition: one that always fails, one that succeeds
Test Steps: Publish the failing message, then the succeeding message, then observe consumption
Expected Result: The failing message is fully retried + DLQ'd BEFORE the next message is even polled; no commit ever skips past an unresolved offset
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka):
1) Push a guaranteed-failing message (KAFKA-020 style), then IMMEDIATELY push a normal valid message
2) App log: confirm the 3 retry attempts + DLQ line for the first message appear BEFORE any 'Processing event' log line for the second message
3) DB: confirm the second (valid) message's submission was still created successfully afterward

Test Case Name: Kafka ingestion + DLQ topics auto-created on startup if missing

Test Steps: Start the consumer against a broker without the topics pre-created
Expected Result: Both analytics.ingestion.raw and analytics.ingestion.raw.dlq are created automatically before polling begins
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka):
1) Delete both topics (or point at a fresh broker), then start the consumer process
2) App log should show 'Kafka topic ... is ready' for both topics
3) List topics (kafka-topics.sh --list or an admin client) and confirm both exist

Test Case Name: Offset commits only after success or confirmed DLQ delivery

2) Push one valid fixture
3) Re-check the offsets -> the committed offset for that partition has advanced by exactly 1

Test Case Name: Raw Kafka payload never appears in plaintext in logs
Preconditions: Any invalid-message path (bad JSON, validation failure, unhandled exception)
Test Steps: Trigger each DLQ/failure path and inspect emitted log lines
Expected Result: Logs show only a sha256 hash + byte length (_payload_fingerprint), never the raw submission content
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka):
1) Push any fixture with a distinctive, searchable value in a text field (e.g. a challenge statement containing 'UNIQUE_MARKER_TEXT') that will fail validation
2) grep app.log / error.log for 'UNIQUE_MARKER_TEXT' -> must return ZERO matches
3) The corresponding log line should instead show 'Payload: sha256:<hash> (<n> bytes)'

Test Case Name: DLQ log/headers surface only non-sensitive identifiers
Preconditions: A message routed to the DLQ
Test Steps: Inspect the DLQ message's Kafka headers and the corresponding log line
Expected Result: submissionId/tenantCode/sessionId are present (for lookup); no submission text or other PII fields appear
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka):
1) Push a fixture that fails validation
2) Consume the DLQ message and print its headers -> expect 'reason', 'submissionId', 'tenantCode', 'sessionId' only
3) Confirm the app log line for this event includes 'Identifiers: submissionId=..., tenantCode=..., sessionId=...' and no raw content

Test Case Name: PII-masking LLM response never logged in full on parse failure
Preconditions: pii_and_abusive_language_detection_activity receiving a malformed (non-JSON) LLM response
Test Steps: Force openrouter_chat_completion to return malformed text containing a fake PII marker
Expected Result: Log line contains only the parse error and response length; the injected marker never appears in logs
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka): This requires developer/staging access to monkeypatch the LLM call — QA can instead confirm the CONTRACT: grep app.log/error.log for 'Failed to parse LLM response JSON' entries and confirm each one is followed only by '(response length=<n>)', never by 'Raw response:' or the literal statement text.

Test Case Name: Thematic classification LLM response never logged in full on parse failure
Preconditions: _run_batched_llm_fallback receiving a malformed LLM response
Test Steps: Force a malformed response containing a fake PII marker during batched fallback
Expected Result: Log line contains only the parse error and response length; marker never appears in logs
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka): Same approach as SEC-003: grep app.log/error.log for 'JSON parsing failed for batched LLM response' and confirm each line ends with '(response length=<n>)' and never includes 'Cleaned response:' or 'Raw response:' content.

Test Case Name: Concurrent process calls on the same pending record are race-safe
Preconditions: A csv_uploads row exists with status='pending'
Test Steps: Fire two POST /v1/process/csv/{record_id} requests for the same pending record at (as close to) the same time
Expected Result: Exactly one request succeeds and starts the workflow; the other gets 409 RecordAlreadyProcessing — try_claim_for_processing's UPDATE ... WHERE status != 'in_progress' RETURNING status is an atomic compare-and-swap, so no double-processing is possible regardless of timing
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka):
1) Create a pending record
2) Fire two curl POST /v1/process/csv/<id> calls in parallel (e.g. via `&` backgrounding in the same shell, both against the same id)
3) Confirm exactly one response is 200 and the other is 409; DB: only one workflow id csv-upload-<id> was ever started (check Temporal UI for a single execution, not two)

Test Case Name: A fully-populated story CSV row still fails schema check on data.pdfUrls.masked (known/accepted gap)
Preconditions: A story CSV row with EVERY column populated, including Pdf/Transcript Link/Blurb/Content/Session ID
Test Steps: Upload + process tests/csv_uploads/valid_story.csv (fully populated)
Expected Result: Row STILL fails pre-publish validation with exactly one problem: "'data.pdfUrls.masked' is missing" — because the CSV-to-payload mapping only ever populates pdfUrls.original (masking happens downstream, after ingestion, per schema.sql's masked_pdf_urls/pii_masked_at columns). This is a known, currently-accepted gap in STORY_KAFKA_SCHEMA (confirmed with the team; schema intentionally left as-is for now) rather than a bug in the CSV pipeline itself.
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka):
Upload+process tests/csv_uploads/valid_story.csv (or any fully-populated story row)
DB: SELECT meta_data->'schema_validation_errors' FROM csv_uploads WHERE id=<id>;  -> exactly one problem, "'data.pdfUrls.masked' is missing"; rows_pushed=0

Test Case Name: Kafka broker unreachable during row push marks record on_hold and raises
Preconditions: A record has passed pre-publish schema validation for at least one row; Kafka broker stopped/unreachable
Test Steps: Process a record whose row(s) would pass schema validation, while Kafka is down
Expected Result: confluent_kafka.Producer.flush()/produce() fails; csv_upload_repo.update_status sets status='on_hold' with stage='Kafka Publishing' in meta_data; the activity re-raises (Temporal will not silently report success)
How to Validate (DB / Logs / Kafka):
1) Stop the Kafka broker
2) Process a record with at least one schema-valid row (would require adjusting STORY_KAFKA_SCHEMA or using a discussion CSV without the pdfUrls.masked gap)
3) DB: SELECT status, meta_data->>'stage' FROM csv_uploads WHERE id=<id>;  -> status='on_hold', stage='Kafka Publishing'
4) Temporal UI: confirm the csv_push_to_kafka_activity attempt shows a failure, not a false success
5) Restart Kafka afterward
